reasoning: I'm voting Congress again, beta. Shantanu Thakur sitting in Delhi with his ministers' chair doesn't change anything for me — he's still the same kind of big person playing games. And Biswajit Das? Switching his shirt like we change our saris? I won't vote for TMC after watching them behave like they own Bangaon. The BJP will shout about law and order, but I don't trust their communal politics either. I voted Congress in 2019 knowing it was wasted, and I felt right about it then. My daughter asked me again if I should vote differently this time, and I told her — no, beta. Voting for the wrong party to keep someone else out won't protect anyone or bring jobs. At least with Congress I can look myself in the mirror and say I didn't vote for thieves or musclemen.
primary_drivers: Maintaining personal integrity and voting with conscience rather than fear
Rejection of TMC's local syndicate culture and corruption (which SSC scam confirmed)
Skepticism of BJP's communal politics despite anti-TMC positioning
